EC BPaaS（AI自動戦略最適化システム）のシステム構成図を作成するために、以下を調査してください：

1. **システム構成図のベストプラクティス**
   - 経営層・非技術者向けに分かりやすい構成図とは
   - 効果的なレイアウト・配色・アイコン使用法
   - 情報の階層化・グルーピング手法

2. **優れたSaaSアーキテクチャ図の例**
   - データフローの可視化方法
   - クラウドサービス連携の表現方法
   - AI/LLM連携の表現方法

3. **nano-banana-pro（Gemini画像生成）での構成図生成**
   - テキストtoイメージで技術図を生成する際のプロンプトベストプラクティス
   - 構成図生成に適したプロンプト構造

調査結果を簡潔にまとめてください。

Here are the search results for AI system architecture diagram visualization, data flow, and cloud services in 2025:

## AI-Powered Architecture Diagram Tools

Several tools are available for creating AI system architecture diagrams with data flow visualization:

### Eraser.io
An easy way to create diagrams is to write out the sequential flow of how data or a request travels through the system (e.g., "User uploads file to web application").(no content)The platform supports 5 diagram types: flow charts, entity relationship diagrams, cloud architecture diagrams, sequence diagrams, and BPMN diagrams.

### Cloudairy
An AI cloud architecture generator is a tool that creates cloud diagrams using artificial intelligence. It reads written descriptions and turns them into visual layouts that show services, networks, and data flow.(no content)The AI cloud architecture diagram generator supports AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid or multi-cloud setups.

### MockFlow
With growing trends in AI-powered diagramming and cloud-native tools, these platforms are becoming increasingly important for efficient design and deployment in 2025 and beyond.(no content)With component packs for popular platforms like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud, teams can quickly assemble architecture diagrams that match real-world implementations. MockFlow supports detailed AWS cloud architecture diagrams, enabling engineers to visualize compute instances, databases, VPCs, and other AWS components.

## Azure AI Architecture

Microsoft's Azure architecture includes virtual networks with several subnets: App Service integration, private endpoint, Foundry integration, Azure AI agent integration, Azure Bastion, jump box, build agents, and Azure firewall. Each subnet hosts specific endpoints or services, like storage, Foundry, Azure AI Search, Azure Cosmos DB, and knowledge store, all connected via private endpoints. Outbound traffic from the network passes through Azure Firewall to reach internet sources.(no content)Managed identities are used to connect Foundry Agent Service to the Foundry project, which in turn accesses Azure OpenAI. Diagrams use numbered green circles to indicate the logical flow, showing how user requests traverse the network and interact with different endpoints.

## Data Architecture Visualization Trends

Emerging trends in data architecture visualization include: Automation tools that auto-generate diagrams from system metadata, integration with data governance for security and compliance measures (GDPR, CCPA), AI tools that analyze data flow patterns and suggest optimizations, and real-time diagramming that reflects current data flows.

## Key Capabilities of AI Diagramming Agents

AI System Architecture Diagraming Agents represent advanced tools designed to streamline the creation and visualization of system architectures. These intelligent agents harness the capabilities of large language models to convert complex system requirements into detailed, comprehensible diagrams. They serve as virtual architects, mapping out the intricate components of software and network systems.

Based on the search results, here are the best practices for prompt engineering with Gemini image generation, particularly for technical diagrams:

## Core Prompting Principles

The fundamental principle is to describe the scene, don't just list keywords. The model's core strength is its deep language understanding. A narrative, descriptive paragraph will almost always produce a better, more coherent image than a simple list of disconnected words.

## For Technical Diagrams Specifically

Gemini's text rendering capabilities allow you to generate images that contain clear and well-placed text, ideal for logos, diagrams, and posters.(no content)Use specific details in your prompts: subject, composition, action, location, and style. Refine prompts with camera angles, lighting, text integration, and factual constraints for diagrams.(no content)Always verify the factual accuracy of data-driven visuals like diagrams and infographics.

## Structured Prompt Elements

You can use specific prompts to achieve consistent characters, precise edits, and blended images. Try using subject, composition, action, location, style, and editing instructions in your prompts to get the best results.

## Technical Controls

Use photographic and cinematic language to control the composition. Terms like wide-angle shot, macro shot, low-angle perspective, 85mm portrait lens, and Dutch angle give you precise control over the final image.(no content)For aspect ratios: Gemini 2.5 Flash Image generally preserves the input image's aspect ratio. If it doesn't, be explicit in your prompt: "Update the input image... Do not change the input aspect ratio."

## General Gemini Prompt Best Practices

Gemini models respond best to prompts that are direct, well-structured, and clearly define the task and any constraints. Be precise and direct: State your goal clearly and concisely. Avoid unnecessary or overly persuasive language.(no content)Use consistent structure: Employ clear delimiters to separate different parts of your prompt. XML-style tags (e.g., `<context>`, `<task>`) or Markdown headings are effective. Choose one format and use it consistently within a single prompt.(no content)With Gemini 3: Less is more - the instruction understanding means you can cut 30-50% of prompt verbosity. Clarity-first: State the goal and format; skip obvious rules. Multimodal strength: Leverage video, image, and audio with simple, specific questions.

## Tools & Resources

There's also an open-source JSON schema validation framework (gemini-image-prompting-handbook) that brings software engineering best practices to prompt engineering, helping transform ad-hoc image generation into reproducible, version-controlled workflows.

Design Principles | Frontegg","url":"https://frontegg.com/guides/saas-applications-architecture-the-how"},{"title":"SaaS Architecture Best Practices in 2025 | by The Algorithm | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@thealgorithm/saas-architecture-best-practices-in-2025-2833f9cdfc75"},{"title":"SaaS architecture: Key principles and best practices - N-iX","url":"https://www.n-ix.com/saas-architecture/"},{"title":"Top 30 SaaS Application Architecture Design Trends in 2025","url":"https://www.peerbits.com/blog/saas-application-architecture-design-trends.html"},{"title":"SaaS Architecture: Essential Principles in 2025","url":"https://www.netguru.com/blog/saas-architecture"},{"title":"SaaS Architecture Fundamentals: Design Principles, Best Practices, And Examples","url":"https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/saas-architecture/"}]

Here are the search results for SaaS architecture diagram best practices for executive non-technical audiences in 2025:

## Key Findings

### General SaaS Architecture Best Practices for 2025

The SaaS landscape continues to evolve rapidly, and businesses face increasing pressure to deliver high-performing, secure, and scalable platforms. In 2025, SaaS architecture isn't just about hosting applications in the cloud — it's about designing future-ready systems that adapt to user needs, embrace automation, and reduce technical debt.

### Architecture Types to Understand

In a single-tenant architecture, each customer (or tenant) has a dedicated instance of the software and database. This model offers enhanced security, as data is isolated from other customers and allows for greater customization. Enterprise-level customers typically choose single-tenant architectures to fulfill specific security, compliance, or performance needs. However, this model can be more expensive due to the need for separate resources for each tenant.(no content)The multi-tenant architecture allows multiple customers to share a single software instance and its underlying infrastructure. Each tenant's data is logically separated to ensure security, but the system operates on shared resources.

### Communicating to Non-Technical Audiences

Low-code and no-code helps non-technical users to build and customize applications without extensive coding knowledge. This democratization of development reduces dependency on traditional programming and accelerates project completion times.

### Strategic Considerations for Executives

In the fast-moving SaaS landscape, architecture isn't just a technical concern — it's a strategic decision that can make or break your product's future. From scalability and speed to user satisfaction and cost-efficiency, everything starts with the right architectural foundation.(no content)According to a recent McKinsey report, scalable architecture is a top-3 technical differentiator for SaaS startups crossing the $10M ARR threshold. Long-term success depends on scalable and secure SaaS architecture, the hidden engine that powers performance, security, and seamless growth.

### Key Design Principles

Monolithic architectures are becoming outdated in the SaaS space. In 2025, modular design and microservices offer superior scalability and maintainability. Microservices allow independent deployment and updates. Smaller services are easier to test, debug, and scale. Teams can work on different services without conflicts.(no content)The right architectural choices enable automatic scaling and reliable performance, turning rapid user growth from a technical challenge into a business opportunity. A well-designed SaaS solution architecture can transform unpredictable infrastructure costs into manageable expenses that grow proportionally with your revenue.

### Real-World Example for Context

When a company lands a big client or a marketing campaign goes viral, the system scales horizontally by adding more instances without requiring expensive emergency upgrades or causing downtime. Consider Zoom's journey: they handled a jump from 10 million to 300 million daily users without rebuilding their platform.

### Planning Best Practices

When approaching the SaaS architecture design process, the journey should start with your story and vision. Before mapping out a SaaS diagram, you need to understand your business model, your market's specific needs, and how you plan to scale and evolve. "Brights' approach to the SaaS architecture design process begins with a detailed analysis of the future product and deep immersion in the business idea to clearly understand the product's scale, target audience, and potential development paths."

The search results focus heavily on technical implementation rather than specifically on creating diagrams for non-technical executive audiences. You may want to consider a follow-up search specifically on "presenting technical architecture to executives" or "simplifying architecture diagrams for business stakeholders" if you need more guidance on the communication and presentation aspects.

Here are the results for AI text-to-image tools for generating system architecture diagrams and infographics:

## AI Diagram Generators for System Architecture

Several AI-powered tools can help you generate system architecture diagrams and infographics from text prompts:

### Popular Tools

1. **Visily** - Visily's Diagram AI lets you quickly generate diagrams for software concepts and system architecture. Use it to create flowcharts, organizational diagrams, network diagrams, mind maps, gantt charts, and more.

2. **Miro AI** - Simply describe your process, system, or concept in text, and watch as their diagramming AI instantly proposes a structured visual layout.(no content)Their AI diagram maker supports flowcharts for business processes, ERDs for database design, mind maps for brainstorming, UML sequence diagrams for system interactions, and UML class diagrams for software architecture.

3. **Eraser (DiagramGPT)** - Five types are currently available – flow charts, entity relationship diagrams, cloud architecture diagrams, sequence diagrams, and BPMN diagrams.(no content)DiagramGPT was created by the team at Eraser, leveraging OpenAI's models.

4. **ToDiagram** - ToDiagram Chat leverages advanced AI technology to convert your text prompts into detailed and accurate diagrams. Whether you need architectural layouts, network diagrams, flowcharts, or other types of visual representations, it makes it easy to create professional-quality diagrams quickly.

5. **ArchGPT** - ArchGPT is an AI-powered diagram generator that creates UML and software architecture diagrams from plain text prompts. It's designed to be fast, simple, and accessible to everyone without needing an account.

6. **Lucidchart AI** - Type text prompts to create AI-generated diagrams automatically on your Lucidchart canvas. Edit your prompt to continue iterating and improving upon the content and flow of your visual with AI.

### Best Practices for Prompts

A one-liner can work, but a clearer diagram usually comes from a 3–5 sentence prompt. Use this approach: Start by listing the main components – services, data stores, APIs, ML models, pipelines...(no content)Group components into layers (e.g., frontend, backend, data, infrastructure). Then describe how these components interact. Explain the runtime flows, such as request handling, background processing, or data movement.(no content)Working with an LLM to create a fleshed out diagram prompt can be effective. Ask it to list out structural components as well as steps of a data or request flow through the system.(no content)If you're in a hurry, instead of writing a prompt from scratch, consider pasting excerpts from existing documents, call transcripts, code (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi, AWS CloudFormation), or uploading image files.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about architecture diagrams for executive presentations and simplifying technical concepts for business stakeholders:

## Key Principles for Creating Architecture Diagrams for Executives

### Know Your Audience
Know your audience and tailor the complexity and details based on who will view the diagram. Executives may prefer high-level visuals, while developers may need more granular technical details.

### Keep It Simple
Keep the diagram as simple as possible while conveying the necessary details. A cluttered diagram with too many animations can confuse your audience.(no content)For stakeholders who might not have a deep technical background, focus on a clear and concise overview. Use simple language and avoid overly technical terms. The goal is to ensure everyone involved receives a clear picture of the software architecture but with the level of detail adjusted for their understanding.

### Use Conceptual Diagrams
A Conceptual Architecture Diagram is a high-level visualization that represents a system's overall structure, boundaries, and interactions with external entities. It provides a bird's-eye view, highlighting key components and their interactions, focusing on the system's purpose and connections rather than technical details. It offers a simplified overview of the system's environment, making it easier to understand and communicate complex systems to diverse stakeholders.

### Benefits for Stakeholder Communication
High-level technical architecture diagrams offer a simplified yet comprehensive representation of system architecture. Diagrams visually represent the system's architecture, making it easier for stakeholders to understand and communicate complex technical concepts.(no content)Architectural diagrams serve as a common language for communication between technical and non-technical stakeholders. They facilitate practical discussions, decision-making, and collaboration among team members, architects, developers, and business stakeholders.

### Best Practices

Keep your diagrams visually clean and avoid cluttering them with unnecessary details. Treat them like visual roadmaps – prioritize the essential elements that showcase the system's core structure and interactions. Use clear shapes, consistent colors, and concise text annotations. This minimalist approach ensures your audience can grasp the overall architecture quickly and efficiently.(no content)A very important tip is to use simple lines, shapes and symbols. An architecture diagram is meant to simplify things, so using complex symbols would just be counterproductive. Consistency in the use of shapes and lines is crucial. The use of color helps in emphasizing a particularly important part of the diagram but otherwise, it is best to keep colors to a minimum.

### Recommended Tools
Excalidraw can be used to create rough sketches of concepts or when explaining a concept to someone else. The tool is intuitive and offers various easy-to-use features and plugins.(no content)Draw.io is useful for drawing AWS architecture diagrams. Draw.io provides various cloud provider icons and templates out of the box, making it easy to include them in architecture designs.

### Templates
Software architecture PowerPoint templates allow you to confidently present technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, development teams, or clients. These are ideal for software engineers, architects, and IT consultants to communicate effectively and professionally.

1→# EC BPaaS 事業化検討 - Messenger会話まとめ

     8→## 会話タイムライン

    10→### 2026/01/10

    17→- AI BPaaSのグローバル競合調査をまとめて共有

    19→- **結論**: GA4ネイティブ連携と中間価格帯（$300-800/月）に参入余地あり
    20→

    22→- Triple Whaleを触ってみる予定
    23→- 「PDCAの自動化ソリューションはない」という認識を確認
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→### 2026/01/12 (9:10)
    28→
    29→#### 永時からの資料共有
    30→- まとめ資料を送付（1ページ、2ページ目を参照）
    31→- Gensparkで生成した資料を添付
    32→
    33→---
    34→
    35→### 2026/01/13 (月曜日 13:42-)
    36→
    37→#### 永時からの提案内容
    38→
    39→**4つの主要コンポーネント**:
    40→
    41→1. **自動戦略最適化**
    42→   - ① ECの状態を把握
    43→   - ② 売上を分解してボトルネックを特定
    44→   - ③ 施策候補を生成
    45→   - ④ 効果・ROIを予測
    46→   - ⑤ 最優先施策を決定
    47→   - ⑥ 実行パラメータを自動生成
    48→   - ⑦ 結果を学習し、次の判断に反映
    49→
    50→2. **自動CRMシナリオ設計**
    51→   - 顧客の状態（行動・購買・LTV・関係性）を定量的に把握
    52→   - 「誰に・いつ・何を・どのチャネルで・どの強さで」届けるかをルール＋AIで自律的に判断・実行
    53→
    54→3. **商品ライン最適化ルール生成**
    55→   - 各商品（SKU／商品群）を定量評価
    56→   - 「残す／伸ばす／統合する／止める」を人の判断に依存せず自動判定
    57→
    58→4. **行動予測モデリング**
    59→   - 過去および現在のユーザー行動データから次に起こる行動の"確率"と"タイミング"を推定
    60→   - 施策判断に活用
    61→
    62→#### 予算・計画の依頼
    63→- 上記コンポーネントの実行に向けた予算と計画作成をAIで依頼
    64→- 早めにローンチさせて事業化を加速したい意向

    67→- **初期予算**: 約200万円
    68→- **優先コンポーネント**: 自動戦略最適化（①〜⑦のフロー）

    73→---
    74→
    75→## 共有された詳細資料: Shopify Plus向けBPaaS KPI横断優先順位付け
    76→
    77→### 1. なぜ「横断した優先順位付け」が必要か
    78→
    79→Shopify Plus規模のECでは、同時に複数の「正しい改善点」が存在:
    80→- CVRが低い
    81→- AOVが低い
    82→- 広告CPAが悪化
    83→- 在庫回転が遅い
    84→- リピート率が落ちている
    85→
    86→**課題**: 全部正しいが、全部は同時にできない
    87→**BPaaSの役割**: 「どれを最初にやるか」を決める
    88→
    89→### 2. 優先順位付けに使う代表的KPI
    90→
    91→| 分類 | KPI |
    92→|------|-----|
    93→| 売上系 | 売上、CVR、AOV |
    94→| 集客系 | セッション数、CPA |
    95→| 顧客系 | 初回→2回目率、LTV |
    96→| オペ系 | 在庫回転率、欠品率 |
    97→| 影響度 | 改修工数、反映スピード |
    98→
    99→### 3. 具体例①: 3つの課題が同時に発生しているケース
   100→
   101→#### 現状データ（GA4＋Shopify）
   102→
   103→| 指標 | 状況 |
   104→|------|------|
   105→| CVR | 0.95%（業界平均1.5%） |
   106→| AOV | 9,800円（安定） |
   107→| 広告CPA | +25%悪化 |
   108→| 在庫 | 商品A 在庫過多 |
   109→| リピート率 | 横ばい |
   110→
   111→#### 人が陥りがちな判断
   112→- 「CVRが低いからサイト改修しよう」
   113→- 「CPAが悪いから広告を止めよう」
   114→- 「在庫が多いからセールしよう」
   115→- → 全部やろうとして失敗
   116→
   117→#### BPaaSによる「横断評価」の考え方
   118→
   119→**Step1: 各KPIの"売上インパクト"を数値化**
   120→
   121→| 課題 | 売上影響 |
   122→|------|----------|
   123→| CVR改善 | +15〜20% |
   124→| CPA改善 | +5〜8% |
   125→| 在庫圧縮 | +3〜5% |
   126→
   127→**Step2: 改善スピード・工数を評価**
   128→
   129→| 課題 | 反映速度 | 工数 |
   130→|------|----------|------|
   131→| CVR改修 | 遅い | 高 |
   132→| CPA調整 | 早い | 低 |
   133→| 在庫施策 | 中 | 中 |
   134→
   135→**Step3: 優先度スコアを算出**
   136→
   137→```
   138→優先度スコア = 売上影響 × 反映スピード ÷ 工数
   139→```
   140→
   141→| 課題 | スコア |
   142→|------|--------|
   143→| CPA調整 | 8.0 |
   144→| CVR改修 | 6.0 |
   145→| 在庫施策 | 4.5 |
   146→
   147→#### BPaaSの結論（人がやらない判断）
   148→
   149→> 今月は「CVR改善」ではなく、まず広告CPAを戻すべき。
   150→
   151→**理由**:
   152→- 即効性が高い
   153→- 工数が小さい
   154→- キャッシュ悪化を防げる
   155→
   156→### 4. 具体例②: CheckoutとCRM、どちらをやるべきか？
   157→
   158→#### 現状
   159→
   160→| KPI | 数値 |
   161→|-----|------|
   162→| Checkout離脱率 | 72% |
   163→| 初回→2回目率 | 11% |
   164→| LTV | 業界平均以下 |
   165→
   166→#### 横断評価
   167→
   168→| 施策 | 売上影響 | 工数 | 再現性 |
   169→|------|----------|------|--------|
   170→| Checkout改善 | 高 | 高 | 中 |
   171→| CRM施策 | 中 | 低 | 高 |
   172→
   173→#### BPaaS判断
   174→
   175→> 先にCRM施策を実行し、Checkout改善は翌四半期に回す
   176→
   177→**理由**:
   178→- PlusのCheckout改修は重い
   179→- CRMは即回せる
   180→- LTV改善が先行すると広告耐性が上がる
   181→
   182→### 5. 生成AIがやっていること（中身）
   183→
   184→- KPI同士の因果関係整理
   185→- 過去改善実績との照合
   186→- 改修リスクの文章化
   187→- 経営判断向け翻訳
   188→
   189→**アウトプット例**:
   190→> 今期はCVR改善よりもLTV改善の方が、利益率とキャッシュフローに与える影響が大きい。
   191→
   192→### 6. Shopifyアプリではできない理由
   193→
   194→- KPIを「同列」にしか見られない
   195→- 事業文脈を理解できない
   196→- 工数・組織負荷を考慮できない
   197→
   198→→ **BPaaSは「数字 × 現実」を横断する**
   199→
   200→### 7. 提案で使える一言
   201→
   202→> KPIは改善すべき指標ではなく、「どれを後回しにするか」を決めるためにある。
   203→
   204→---
   205→
   206→## 次のアクション
   207→
   208→1. **松﨑**: 社内検討後、計画と概算見積を作成
   209→2. **優先開発**: 自動戦略最適化（①〜⑦フロー）
   210→3. **初期予算**: 約200万円でのMVP開発
   211→
   212→---
   213→
   214→## 関連資料
   215→
   216→- グローバル競合調査（15社比較）
   217→- Genspark生成資料（1-2ページ目）
   218→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
調査完了しました。以下に結果をまとめます。

---

## 調査結果まとめ

### 1. 経営層・非技術者向けシステム構成図のベストプラクティス

#### レイアウト・デザイン原則

**シンプルさを最優先**
- [vFunction](https://vfunction.com/blog/architecture-diagram-guide/)によると、クラッタ（ごちゃつき）を避け、必要最低限の要素だけを表示
- [Medium](https://medium.com/art-of-data-engineering/mastering-architecture-diagrams-and-technical-presentations-a-data-architects-guide-eb410b49bc19)では「視覚的なロードマップ」として扱い、コア構造と相互作用を優先的に表示

**階層化・グルーピング**
- [MockFlow](https://mockflow.com/blog/What-is-Architectural-Diagramming)では「概念図（Conceptual Diagram）」を推奨：技術詳細ではなくシステムの目的と接続に焦点
- フロントエンド／バックエンド／データ／インフラの4層構造が一般的

**配色・アイコン**
- 色は強調したい部分だけに限定使用
- シンプルな線・形状・記号を使用（複雑なシンボルは逆効果）
- 一貫性のある形状と線が重要

#### 非技術者向けのポイント

- 専門用語を避け、シンプルな言語で説明
- 「鳥瞰図（bird's-eye view）」的な視点で全体像を提示
- [Bizzdesign](https://bizzdesign.com/blog/technical-architecture-diagrams)では「共通言語」として図を活用し、意思決定・議論・コラボレーションを促進

---

### 2. SaaS/AIアーキテクチャ図の優れた表現方法

#### データフローの可視化

- [Eraser.io](https://www.eraser.io/ai/architecture-diagram-generator)：データや要求がシステムを通過する順序を記述
- [Microsoft Azure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/)：番号付き緑丸で論理フローを示す手法
- [Cloudairy](https://cloudairy.com/ai/ai-cloud-architecture-generator)：サービス、ネットワーク、データフローを視覚的レイアウトで表現

#### クラウドサービス連携の表現

- AWS/Azure/GCPの公式アイコンセットを使用
- [Draw.io](https://app.diagrams.net/)はクラウドプロバイダーアイコンとテンプレートを標準装備
- プライベートエンドポイント、ファイアウォール、VPCを明示

#### AI/LLM連携の表現

- AIコンポーネントは独立したボックスで明示
- データ入力→AI処理→出力の流れを矢印で表現
- Azure例：マネージドIDでFoundry Agent Service → OpenAIを接続

---

### 3. nano-banana-pro（Gemini画像生成）での構成図生成

#### プロンプトベストプラクティス

[Google Developers Blog](https://developers.googleblog.com/en/how-to-prompt-gemini-2-5-flash-image-generation-for-the-best-results/)と[Gemini公式Tips](https://blog.google/products/gemini/prompting-tips-nano-banana-pro/)より：

**基本原則**
- **キーワード羅列ではなく、シーンを描写する**：ナラティブな記述が効果的
- **具体的な詳細を含める**：主題、構成、アクション、場所、スタイル
- **3〜5文程度**の説明が最適（1行だけでは不十分）

**構成図向けプロンプト構造**

```
[図の種類と目的]
A clean, professional system architecture diagram for [サービス名]

[主要コンポーネントの列挙]
showing the following components: 
- [コンポーネント1]: [簡単な説明]
- [コンポーネント2]: [簡単な説明]
...

[データフロー/接続の説明]
with data flowing from [開始点] through [中間処理] to [終点]

[視覚スタイル指定]
Style: modern, minimalist, using soft gradients and rounded rectangles, 
color scheme of [メインカラー] and [アクセントカラー], 
suitable for executive presentation
```

**注意点**
- [Gemini Promptingガイド](https://www.promptingguide.ai/models/gemini)：Geminiはテキストレンダリングが得意でロゴ・図・ポスターに最適
- ただし、**ファクト情報（数値・ラベル）の正確性は必ず検証**が必要
- 複雑な技術図は生成AIより**専用ダイアグラムツール**（Eraser, Miro AI, ArchGPT）の方が適している場合あり

#### 代替アプローチ（推奨）

技術図の生成には、画像生成AIよりも**テキスト→図変換AI**が高精度：

| ツール | 特徴 |
|--------|------|
| [Eraser DiagramGPT](https://www.eraser.io/diagramgpt) | フロー、ER図、クラウド構成図対応 |
| [Miro AI](https://miro.com/ai/diagram-ai/) | フローチャート、UML、マインドマップ |
| [ArchGPT](https://www.archgpt.in/) | UML、ソフトウェアアーキテクチャ図 |
| [Visily](https://www.visily.ai/diagram-ai/) | ネットワーク図、組織図対応 |

---

### EC BPaaS構成図への適用案

EC BPaaSの「自動戦略最適化」7ステップフローを図示する場合：

**推奨レイアウト**
```
[データソース層]
GA4 / Shopify / BigQuery
      ↓
[AI分析層]
状態把握 → ボトルネック特定 → 施策生成
      ↓
[意思決定層]
ROI予測 → 優先順位決定 → 実行パラメータ生成
      ↓
[実行・学習層]
施策実行 → 結果学習 → フィードバックループ
```

**経営層向けの強調ポイント**
- 「人手vs自動化」の対比を視覚化
- 「何が自動化されるか」を明確に
- ROI/コスト削減効果を図内に数値で表示

---

### Sources

- [Acropolium - SaaS Architecture Best Practices](https://acropolium.com/blog/saas-app-architecture-2022-overview/)
- [Medium - Mastering Architecture Diagrams](https://medium.com/art-of-data-engineering/mastering-architecture-diagrams-and-technical-presentations-a-data-architects-guide-eb410b49bc19)
- [vFunction - Architecture Diagram Guide](https://vfunction.com/blog/architecture-diagram-guide/)
- [MockFlow - Architectural Diagramming](https://mockflow.com/blog/What-is-Architectural-Diagramming)
- [Bizzdesign - Technical Architecture Diagrams](https://bizzdesign.com/blog/technical-architecture-diagrams)
- [Eraser.io - AI Architecture Diagram Generator](https://www.eraser.io/ai/architecture-diagram-generator)
- [Microsoft Azure AI Architecture](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/)
- [Google Developers - Gemini Image Generation Prompts](https://developers.googleblog.com/en/how-to-prompt-gemini-2-5-flash-image-generation-for-the-best-results/)
- [Gemini Prompting Tips](https://blog.google/products/gemini/prompting-tips-nano-banana-pro/)
- [Cloudairy - AI Cloud Architecture Generator](https://cloudairy.com/ai/ai-cloud-architecture-generator)
